notes: Canonical M-E encoding (zero=100), sign+magnitude+cond_neg, no sharing
Shared K×shift precomputation, still uses subtractor for E adjustment
Direct formula m_i = nmc×sh_i | k3×sh_{i+1} | k9×sh_{i+2} | k9×sh_{i-1}. No subtractor. Zero=100 encoding, 9-bit zero masking.
Zero=000 encoding. K-flag masking with nz (3 gates) vs 9-bit output masking (10 gates). nz detect costs 5 gates (vs 9). r8=m0 pass-through saves 1 cond_neg gate.
Same as v4 + sh6=u11 optimization: since max S=6=3+3, u11=AND(s2,s1)=1 forces s0=0, so sh6=u11 saves 1 AND gate.
Prefix-OR formula for cond_neg: result_i = XOR(m_i, AND(sign, OR(m_0..m_{i-1}))). Replaces carry chain (22 gates) with prefix-OR chain + AND + XOR (20 gates). Saves 2 gates.

prefix_or_7 = OR(m0..m6) = nz for all achievable FP4 products (proof: all 18 non-zero magnitudes have at least 1 bit in positions 0..6; magnitude 144 has m4=1 as well as m7). Therefore sp7=AND(sign,p7)=AND(sign,nz)=res0, eliminating p7 (1 OR) and sp7 (1 AND). Saves 2 gates.

Cirbo CircuitFinderSat exact synthesis proves S-decoder (3-in 7-out) optimal at 11 gates in AND/OR/XOR/NOT basis (N=8,9 UNSAT; N=11 SAT). New 11-gate decoder: 2 OR + 1 NOT + 4 AND + 4 XOR. Saves 2 gates vs 13-gate hand-crafted (3 NOT + 10 AND).
